Transcrição Completa: How to Make Millions on Social Media - Brandon Carter
[00:00] All right. Um, Jeremy asked me to talk to you guys about social media. I obviously would have preferred to talk about Adderall and Medafanil as usual, but apparently you guys want to hear about social media.
[00:12] Um, I'm not sure why he asked me to talk about it because I only got a million YouTube subscribers and a million Instagram followers and like only like half a million Tik Tok followers. So, I don't know. I don't know if that qualifies me as an expert, but I'll do the best I can.
[00:28] So, we're going to talk about how to make millions on social media. And I definitely have made millions off it. Like I'm not one of these folks who like want to be famous for like props or like all the other stuff. I ain't a teenage girl, you know what I'm saying? Trying to do this for validation.
[00:45] Like this is 100% for money. I swear to God, I wouldn't make one YouTube video. I wouldn't post. I wouldn't even have a social media. I wouldn't even started the page if I couldn't make money off it. Right? So that's what we're going to talk about.
[01:00] Um I made my own slides like right before I came. So, there's going to be a lot of misspellings and typos. All right. And all types of other fuckups that I'm not even fully aware of yet. It's okay, though. I'm I'm I have dyslexia. Like, I've been diagnosed with dyslexia. If any of y'all make fun of me for it, it's a hate crime. And God will punish you for this. All right. When I say God, I mean Zuckerberg.

[16:15] I'm just going to throw stuff out every day, as much as possible, and then all those views start to add up. Does that make sense?

[23:20] And um everyone was thinking it, but yo, my boy, man, so he was with this girl, right? And uh and this girl was going around telling everybody that he like forced himself on her, right?
[23:35] But then he showed me like text from her that day, like the day. He's like, "Man, she text me today. She's texting me nudes today. You know what I'm saying? So, he had to like have he had to have his lawyer send her a cease and desist.
[23:50] That happens, man. Like, you heard about that baseball player? Uh, you know, what's his name?
[23:58] AUDIÊNCIA: Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah.
[24:00] Chick said that he like beat her or something. But then there she posted videos of her at his crib like right before she left smiling. There was text messages of her telling her her homies that she was going to try to u she called him his next her next victim, right?
[24:18] Like that happens out here, man. So it's like it's dangerous. You become like if you a regular guy, nobody gives a care right? But when you become like kind of rich and famous, you got a reputation. Now you a target to some of these chicks, you know, straight up like watch out for that.
[24:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Be Mentally Strong"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Aviso final sobre preparação psicológica
[24:38] Um yeah. And just in in general, you want to make sure you're super mentally strong before you do this because I've seen people uh get a little bit of fame and kind of go crazy. You know what I'm saying?
[24:52] I've seen some people go crazy uh more than once, like more than once they they lost it. They lost like touch with reality and it's not uncommon. It's actually not uncommon. Like people kind of lose it.
[25:05] And I I think it's because it brings all your insecurities to the forefront. You know, the human brain was not designed to get that much criticism from that many people, right?
[25:18] Like historically, we were in tribes of like 100-200 people, right? And if you were ostracized from the tribe, you would die. You get killed by another animal or another tribe or something.
[25:32] So our ancestors really tried to fit in with those tribes, right? And and the people who did pass their DNA down and we're all descendants of them, right? So it's hardwired to respond poorly to criticism. You know what I mean?
[25:48] And the only way to combat that is to be super sure of yourself and just be super like it's going to sound gay, but you got to like really like yourself. You know what I'm saying?
[26:00] Cuz because if you like don't mess with yourself and then you got hundreds or thousands of people talking about you, it's going to like that part of your brain that doesn't mess with yourself is going to take over the rest. I've seen this happen mad times, you know?
[26:18] So, you got to really like the person you are. Does that make sense? Yeah. Cuz if you have any other stuff going on, you ashamed of something, you out here.
[26:30] Yo. All right. Yo, I'm going tell you a story. So, there's this dude. There's this dude. I'm not going to say his name um because it's just it wouldn't be it wouldn't be cool.
[26:42] But he he's he's a homosexual, right? But like don't nobody know. The reason I know is because he was on his story talking about, "Man, Google thinks I'm gay. It keeps showing me these ads for gay cruise lines." And I'm like-
[26:58] I don't get them man. Ain't nothing wrong with being gay, but it's like because he was like ashamed of it. You know what I'm saying? Like like I ain't homophobic, man. If any guys want to stop being with girls, make it easier for us, man. You know what I'm saying? That's cool with me, you know?
[27:15] Um, but it was messing with him. You know what I'm saying? He had there something he had to hide. And I was like, I ain't say nothing, but I was like, uh, I know how algorithms work. I know I ran ads before. You know what I'm saying? I I ran ads before. I know.
[27:32] And I asked one of my boys, this guy named Matt Galant. He's like an OG, one of the best I'm sorry, Google ads guys like ever. Like he's one of the OGs. Uh he told me this year, man, his company about to do 50 million.
[27:48] But anyway, he I was like, yo, man, this mean he gay, right? He been searching gay stuff. He was like, it appears so. It appears so. I was like, all right. I mean, it's cool, like whatever. But it was messing with him.
[28:05] And then maybe like later that year, I'll never forget this because it was like a crazy night. I was in Austin, Texas. I was in a diner and I had just met Tim Ferriss for like no reason. Like it was just it was a crazy night.
[28:20] And then right after right after I meet Tim Ferriss, I get a call from this guy and it's like, "Yo, it's like 2:00 in the morning." I'm like, "This is weird." Hoping ain't no gay stuff. Not I mean, you could be gay, but like not with me.
[28:35] So anyway, I take the call. He's like, "Yo, can I talk to you?" He's like, "Yeah." And he's crying. He's telling me he can't stop doing cocaine. And this guy's like rich and famous. He can't stop doing cocaine.
[28:50] And he's like, "He's got all these problems." And I'm like, he was also getting a lot of criticism online at the same time simultaneously. And I really think that like him being ashamed of him being ashamed not not that not that there's anything wrong with being gay and stuff. It's just but he was ashamed of he was scared to like you know reveal that.
[29:12] Um I think holding that back kind of messed with him and it like I was like damn I don't know that that made me sad for him. You know what I'm saying?
[29:25] And I've seen he didn't go crazy. He was able to pull it together but I've seen people legit lose their mind. I know this one guy who's been in the mental hospital for like about a year. It's going on a year.
[29:40] He went on a podcast, got like and it got a lot of views. He he I don't think he was ready for that kind of attention. He he went super crazy. He's been in mental hospital for a year.
[29:52] So, just be sure you're ready for that mentally. If you don't if there's some stuff going on in your life or in your personality or some stuff you hiding, like you ain't going to be able to hide that. You know what I'm saying?
[30:05] Or it's going to manifest itself in some kind of way. So, if you don't if you don't really mess with yourself, like if you if you like secretly gay, just come out first and stuff. You know what I'm saying?
[30:18] Or like if you be like doing stuff or watching gay porn and stuff you got to either like stop or like or or like start being cool with it. You know what I'm saying?
[30:30] Or like some anything whatever it is that you don't mess with, you got to like really come come to grips with that because uh it's going to put a real it's going to it's going to mess with you if you get popular. You know what I'm saying?
[30:45] And that's all I got. What questions you have for me?
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
1 milhão de seguidores no YouTube
1 milhão de seguidores no Instagram
500 mil seguidores no TikTok
$5 milhões por mês (caso Andrew Tate - orgânico, sem ads)
$12,000 - preço do produto principal
Centenas de milhares de dólares por mês via estratégia de DM no Instagram
59% taxa de fechamento em chamadas de vendas
3.9 ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) em 10 dias
29.5% ROAS em outra campanha
80-100 vídeos filmados em 2 dias
Filmagem a cada 2 meses
2-3 TikToks por dia
2-3 YouTube Shorts por dia
Lock Rule: $1,000 de venda = 1 hora de conteúdo assistido
2 anos recomendados antes de desistir
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Monetização:
Funil de Lead Magnet: Conteúdo → Lead Magnet gratuito → Aplicação → Chamada de vendas → Venda ($12k)
Estratégia de DM no Instagram: Conteúdo → "DM a palavra 'Freedom'" → Setters respondem → Chamada de vendas → Venda
Retargeting Orgânico + Pago: Conteúdo no YouTube → Retarget quem assistiu vídeos específicos → Aplicação → Venda
Crescimento:
Long Tail Strategy: Quantidade sobre qualidade - múltiplas postagens diárias somam mais que tentativas virais
Batching de Conteúdo: Filmar 80-100 vídeos em 2 dias a cada 2 meses
Shoutouts Pagos: Apenas no Instagram, através de agência especializada
Postagem Consistente: Todo dia em todas as plataformas (YouTube, TikTok, Instagram)
Múltiplas Postagens Diárias: 2-3 por plataforma
Lead Magnet Específico:
"The Ten Commandments of Online Training" (20 páginas)
Thank you page com vídeo + aplicação
Email forçado para abrir (melhora deliverability)
Cada mandamento: ensina O QUE fazer, mas não COMO
Exemplos de estudantes com antes/depois
Links para entrevistas com estudantes
Aplicações múltiplas ao longo do conteúdo
ESTRUTURA:
Modelo de Negócio:
Nicho: Ensinar personal trainers a construir negócios fitness online
Produto: $12,000
100% vendas via chamadas ao vivo (não automated)
Time de setters para qualificação
Time de closers para vendas
Produção de Conteúdo:
Terceirização de edição e postagem desde o início
Batching a cada 2 meses
Mudança de camisas e relógios para variedade visual
Lista de ideias preparada antes da gravação
Foco em volume, não perfeição
Preparação Mental:
Não ler comentários
Ter certeza de si mesmo antes de começar
Resolver inseguranças pessoais previamente
Estar preparado para críticas em massa
Aceitar aspectos de si mesmo que possam ser julgados
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Milhões de dólares por ano em receita total
Centenas de milhares por mês apenas da estratégia de Instagram DM
ROAS de 3.9 e 29.5% em campanhas pagas
Produto principal: $12,000
Economia potencial em comissões se conseguir vender sem chamadas ao vivo
Investimento em shoutouts pagos no Instagram (valor não especificado)
LADO SOMBRIO/CUSTOS:
Questões de segurança pessoal (fans muito entusiasmados)
Haters com milhões de seguidores fazendo vídeos de 40 minutos de crítica
Perda de privacidade
Atenção sexual indesejada/perigosa
Risco de falsas acusações
Saúde mental (pessoas perdendo contato com a realidade)
Necessidade de força mental extrema
Trauma histórico de ostracização tribal amplificado pela internet
DURAÇÃO: ~31 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação ao vivo para grupo de empreendedores/marketers
